name: subdomains-top1million-5000.txt
purpose: The 5 000 most common subdomain names from the top-1-million set. A baseline fast list for initial DNS enumeration.
whenToUse: When you need the fastest possible run over the most popular subdomains or are short on time/resolvers.
tool: ffuf / gobuster dns / puredns / dnsx / shuffledns

name: subdomains-top1million-20000.txt
purpose: The 20 000 most common subdomains. A medium-sized list, a good balance between speed and coverage.
whenToUse: The standard choice for subdomain enumeration, when top-5000 is already too small but the full list is excessive.
tool: ffuf / gobuster dns / puredns / dnsx / shuffledns

name: subdomains-top1million-110000.txt
purpose: The 110 000 most used subdomains (exported from a Cloudflare zone). A large list for deep enumeration.
whenToUse: When you need more complete coverage after the fast lists and you have reliable resolvers.
tool: ffuf / gobuster dns / puredns / dnsx / shuffledns

name: bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt
purpose: The top 100 000 subdomains from bitquark's research on DNS data. Differs from top1million in composition and complements it well.
whenToUse: As an additional large list to catch subdomains absent from the top1million set.
tool: ffuf / gobuster dns / puredns / dnsx / shuffledns

name: dns-Jhaddix.txt
purpose: A copy in SecLists of Jason Haddix's famous all.txt - a union of the wordlists of every DNS tool. Very broad enumeration.
whenToUse: For aggressive, exhaustive subdomain brute-forcing when you need maximum coverage in a single run.

name: all.txt (Jhaddix gist)
purpose: Jason Haddix's original gist "all wordlists from every dns enumeration tool ever" - the source of dns-Jhaddix.txt.
whenToUse: When you need the original all.txt straight from the author rather than the copy in SecLists.

name: n0kovo_subdomains.txt
purpose: A highly effective subdomain wordlist of ~3 million lines, collected from TLS certificates across the entire IPv4 range. In SecLists it ships as a single file.
whenToUse: For deep brute-forcing when the standard lists are exhausted; especially effective against targets with many subdomains.

name: n0kovo_subdomains (repository: tiny/small/medium/large/huge)
purpose: The original n0kovo repository with ready-made size slices of the wordlist (tiny, small, medium, large, huge) for matching your resolver budget.
whenToUse: When you need a specific n0kovo size slice (for example, medium for balance) instead of the single large file from SecLists.
size: from a few thousand to ~3 000 000 lines (depending on the variant)

name: best-dns-wordlist.txt (assetnote)
purpose: Assetnote's flagship DNS wordlist for the most thorough brute-forcing; helps find very rare subdomains.
whenToUse: For the deepest DNS enumeration, when you have powerful resolvers and time; the final "heavy" run.

name: 2m-subdomains.txt (assetnote)
purpose: Assetnote subdomain wordlist of ~2.1 million lines, generated by Commonspeak2. A more size-manageable alternative to best-dns-wordlist.
whenToUse: When you need a strong but not gigantic subdomain wordlist from Assetnote for routine enumeration.

name: dnsmap.txt (Kali)
purpose: A small subdomain wordlist built into Kali (a symlink to wordlist_TLAs.txt from the dnsmap package). Used by DNS tools by default.
whenToUse: For quick out-of-the-box checks on Kali, for example as the wordlist for dnsrecon/dnsmap without downloading SecLists.

name: namelist.txt
purpose: A compact list of common hostnames from SecLists Discovery/DNS, handy for brute-forcing virtual hosts via the Host header.
whenToUse: For fast VHost fuzzing, when you need a short, general list of names against a single IP.

name: subdomains-top1million-5000.txt (as a VHost list)
purpose: The same DNS subdomain lists are reused for VHost fuzzing: the value is substituted into the Host header (ffuf -H "Host: FUZZ.target"). The small list is for a quick start.
whenToUse: A first fast run over virtual hosts on a single IP, to catch common names like dev/staging/admin.

name: subdomains-top1million-20000.txt (as a VHost list)
purpose: A medium DNS list, reused for enumerating virtual hosts via the Host header. Good coverage for a reasonable time.
whenToUse: The main VHost fuzzing run, when top-5000 produced no results but the full lists are excessive.

name: fierce-hostlist.txt
purpose: A small list of hostnames from the wordlist of the fierce tool (part of SecLists). Compact and good for enumerating virtual hosts.
whenToUse: When you need a short, targeted list for VHost fuzzing in addition to namelist.txt.
